title: penstemon subglaber
intro: Penstemon subglaber, also known as Downy Beardtongue, is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the plantain family (Plantaginaceae). This perennial herb is native to the southwestern United States and has been used by indigenous communities for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. The plant contains compounds with anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of bronchitis, asthma, and other respiratory conditions. In herbal preparations, Penstemon subglaber is often used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to promote healthy breathing and respiratory function.
